source_jobs.title: Senior VAT Manager
source_jobs.description:
About Unity Advisory

Unity Advisory is a pure-play, experience-led CFO advisory firm providing integrated finance, commercial, tax and deals support. We are free from audit conflicts and AI-enabled by design, combining deep technical expertise with automation, analytics and AI built into the delivery of every engagement. We are building an alternative to traditional advisory firms, focused on clarity over complexity, outcomes over hours, and practical advice delivered at pace for CFOs making high-consequence decisions.



The Role

As a result of Unity Advisory's growth and success, we are looking to hire a Senior VAT Manager. You will have experience in working in a fast paced and complex environment and be collaborative to find the right solution for your clients. You will be highly client centric and able to build long lasting relationships which are valued by your clients. You will also be proactive in taking value enhancing solutions to your clients in both tax and more broadly. This is a rare opportunity for a dynamic and commercially minded adviser to shape the future of our tax practice in a lean and agile environment.

 

Responsibilities

 - Provide expert VAT advisory services to clients, ensuring compliance with UK, EU and global indirect tax regulations. 

 - Develop and implement VAT strategies for clients that align with commercial drivers. 

 - Build strong and trusted relationships with clients. 

 - Work with leading experts across a range of advisory areas in multi-disciplinary teams. 

 - Proactively use data and analysis tools to identify opportunities and risks for clients. 

 - Liaise with HMRC and other tax authorities on VAT-related matters. 

 - Stay updated on changes in VAT legislation and advise clients on the implications. 

 - Train and mentor junior staff on VAT compliance and advisory matters. 

 

What You'll bring:

 - Professional qualification in tax (e.g., CTA, ATT) or equivalent experience. 

 - Extensive knowledge of UK and EU VAT regulations and compliance requirements. 

 - Experience to identify and lead technology enabled solutions to client issues.

 - Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with attention to detail. 

 - Excellent communication and interpersonal skills to effectively liaise with clients and tax authorities 



Work Environment 

A truly hybrid and flexible working environment. We offer the opportunity to be at the forefront of AI-driven advisory services. You’ll be working on cutting-edge projects and encouraged to deploy the latest analytical tools and approaches.  This is a fantastic opportunity to join a fast growing business and shape your career. 

 

Additional Information 
 
At Unity Advisory, we are committed to providing an inclusive and accessible recruitment process.  In line with the Equality Act 2010, we will accommodate any suitable candidate requiring assistance to attend or conduct an interview. If you need any adjustments or support, please let us know when either scheduling your interview or in your application cover letter. We are dedicated to ensuring that everyone has an equal opportunity to succeed and are here to support you throughout the process. 

 

PLEASE BE ADVISED THAT WE DO NOT ACCEPT ANY UNSOLICITED CVS FROM THIRD PARTIES

source_jobs.title: Director- Financial Due Diligence
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT UNITY ADVISORY

Unity Advisory is a pure-play, experience-led CFO advisory firm providing integrated finance, commercial, tax and deals support. We are free from audit conflicts and AI-enabled by design, combining deep technical expertise with automation, analytics and AI built into the delivery of every engagement. We are building an alternative to traditional advisory firms, focused on clarity over complexity, outcomes over hours, and practical advice delivered at pace for CFOs making high-consequence decisions.




THE ROLE

We are seeking an entrepreneurial and commercially astute Director to lead our growing Financial Due Diligence and Value Creation practice. This senior leadership role is responsible for driving client relationships, originating and executing engagements, and scaling a differentiated FDD platform powered by AI.

You’ll bring a strong track record in FDD leadership from a Big 4 or top-tier advisory environment, with proven experience advising Private Equity and corporate clients on complex transactions. As a Director, you will play a pivotal role in shaping Unity Advisory’s market presence, mentoring senior talent, and developing innovative, outcome-driven solutions for clients.

This is a hybrid leadership role based in our Mayfair office, offering the opportunity to build and lead one of the most forward-thinking FDD practices in the market.




Responsibilities

 - Originate, lead, and deliver high-impact buy-side and sell-side due diligence engagements.

 - Partner with Private Equity and corporate clients to provide board-level insights on deal value, risks, and opportunities.

 - Drive post-deal value creation strategies, including operational improvements, working capital optimisation, and performance acceleration.

 - Leverage AI, automation, and data analytics to create innovative client solutions.

 - Lead business development, pitches, and thought leadership to grow Unity Advisory’s FDD market share.

 - Build and nurture a high-performing team, coaching Senior Managers and Managers to become future leaders.

 - Collaborate with the wider Unity Advisory leadership team to evolve firm strategy and service offerings.

 - Represent Unity Advisory in the market, building a trusted advisor profile with clients, investors, and partners.


QUALIFICATIONS

 - Minimum 10+ years’ experience in Financial Due Diligence/Transaction Services, with senior leadership exposure.

 - Proven track record of originating and delivering large, complex transactions.

 - Extensive experience with Private Equity clients and portfolio company situations.

 - Strong financial and commercial skills, with the ability to influence senior stakeholders.

 - Demonstrated ability to develop and lead high-performing teams.

 - Entrepreneurial mindset and appetite to help build a challenger firm from the ground up.

 - Qualified accountant (ACA, ACCA, CIMA or equivalent) highly preferred.


ADDITIONAL INFORMATION

At Unity Advisory, we are committed to providing an inclusive and accessible recruitment process. In line with the Equality Act 2010, we will accommodate any suitable candidate requiring assistance to attend or conduct an interview. If you need any adjustments or support, please let us know when either scheduling your interview or in your application cover letter. We are dedicated to ensuring that everyone has an equal opportunity to succeed and are here to support you throughout the process.



PLEASE BE ADVISED THAT WE DO NOT ACCEPT ANY UNSOLICITED CVS FROM THIRD PARTIES

source_jobs.title: Director - Corporate Tax
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT UNITY ADVISORY

Unity Advisory is a pure-play, experience-led CFO advisory firm providing integrated finance, commercial, tax and deals support. We are free from audit conflicts and AI-enabled by design, combining deep technical expertise with automation, analytics and AI built into the delivery of every engagement. We are building an alternative to traditional advisory firms, focused on clarity over complexity, outcomes over hours, and practical advice delivered at pace for CFOs making high-consequence decisions.




THE ROLE

Are you a seasoned tax advisory professional who thrives on innovation and cutting‑edge technology? Unity Advisory is seeking a forward‑thinking Corporate Tax Director to elevate our client service offerings and drive tax excellence with precision, efficiency, and strategic insight.




RESPONSIBILITIES



 - Lead and deliver strategic corporate tax advisory services to clients, focusing on complex tax planning, structuring advice, and optimisation strategies.

 - Provide expert guidance on tax-efficient business structures, cross-border transactions, and M&A tax implications.

 - Develop and implement technology‑enabled tax advisory solutions to enhance analytical capabilities, scenario modeling, and strategic decision-making.

 - Drive business growth through targeted client development activities, thought leadership, and building trusted advisor relationships.

 - Interpret and navigate complex tax legislation—identifying planning opportunities and advising clients on strategic tax positions.

 - Design and implement robust tax governance frameworks to optimise tax positions while managing compliance and reputational risks.

 - Coordinate with clients' tax compliance teams and other advisors to ensure alignment between tax strategy and execution.

 - Lead multidisciplinary advisory teams, fostering innovation and excellence in tax consulting services.

 - Deliver compelling, high-impact tax insights and recommendations to C-suite executives, enabling strategic business decisions.

 - Create influential thought leadership content on emerging tax matters to position Unity Advisory as a leading authority in corporate tax advisory.

 - Identify emerging market trends and develop innovative tax advisory service offerings to address evolving client needs.


QUALIFICATIONS

 - ACA, ACCA, CTA Qualified with 10+ years in corporate tax, including experience at leading accountancy firms.

 - In‑depth knowledge of UK and international tax regimes, statutes, and emerging trends.

 - Tech‑savvy: experienced with tax technology platforms and data‑driven tax solutions.

 - Track record of successful business development and client relationship management

 - Strong analytical prowess and problem‑solving ability—adept at navigating complex client scenarios and transforming them into tax‑efficient outcomes.

 - Exceptional communicator: able to simplify complex tax matters for non‑experts and influence at the executive level.

 - Leadership mindset: proactive, collaborative, able to inspire and develop high‑performing client service teams.

 - Composed, commercial, and ethical—delivering results under deadline pressure while maintaining integrity.

 - Experience in managing multiple client engagements simultaneously while maintaining quality and client satisfaction.

 - Track record of successful business development and client relationship management.




ADDITIONAL INFORMATION

At Unity Advisory, we are committed to providing an inclusive and accessible recruitment process. In line with the Equality Act 2010, we will accommodate any suitable candidate requiring assistance to attend or conduct an interview. If you need any adjustments or support, please let us know when either scheduling your interview or in your application cover letter. We are dedicated to ensuring that everyone has an equal opportunity to succeed and are here to support you throughout the process.



PLEASE NOTE: We do not accept unsolicited

source_jobs.title: Senior Manager - Financial Due Diligence
source_jobs.description:
SENIOR MANAGER – FINANCIAL DUE DILIGENCE

Location: Mayfair/ Hybrid
Type: Permanent



About Unity Advisory

Unity Advisory is a pure-play, experience-led CFO advisory firm providing integrated finance, commercial, tax and deals support. We are free from audit conflicts and AI-enabled by design, combining deep technical expertise with automation, analytics and AI embedded into the delivery of every engagement. We are building an alternative to traditional advisory firms, focused on clarity over complexity, outcomes over hours, and practical advice delivered at pace for CFOs making high-consequence decisions.

Our culture is deliberately simple and high-performance: Straightforward, Commercial, Open, Experimental and Team-First.




THE ROLE

We are seeking a commercially minded and analytically strong Senior Manager to join our growing FDD and Value Capture team. This high-impact role will work closely with our corporate clients, and internal leadership to deliver complex due diligence engagements and post-deal value creation programmes.

You’ll bring proven experience from a Big 4 or leading consultancy firm, with a track record of managing end-to-end buy-side and sell-side FDD assignments. As a senior member of the team, you’ll lead engagements, develop talent, and drive the evolution of our AI-enabled FDD offering. Helping clients make better, faster investment decisions and deliver post-deal value. This is a hybrid role based in our Mayfair office and offers the opportunity to shape the direction of a modern, tech-enabled diligence and value creation practice from the ground up.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

The working environment at Unity Advisory is fast-paced and entrepreneurial. You will be involved in a wide range of activities including:

 - Lead complex buy-side and sell-side financial due diligence engagements across a variety of sectors.

 - Oversee end-to-end project delivery, from initial scoping to reporting, stakeholder presentations, and recommendations.

 - Partner with Private Equity and corporate clients to assess deal drivers, KPIs, underlying earnings, working capital, and cash flows.

 - Identify and quantify risks, synergies, and improvement opportunities during both diligence and post-deal phases.

 - Support and develop value capture plans, including margin enhancement, pricing strategies, working capital optimisation, and operational efficiency.

 - Collaborate with internal data and AI specialists to integrate advanced analytics and automation into our FDD delivery model.

 - Develop and maintain strong relationships with investors, management teams, and third-party advisors.

 - Contribute to business development activities, proposals, and thought leadership.

 - Mentor junior team members and contribute to shaping a high-performing, growth-focused team culture.


WHAT YOU BRING

 - Minimum 6+ years of experience in Financial Due Diligence or Transaction Services from a Big 4 or top-tier consultancy environment.

 - Proven ability to lead multiple transaction engagements simultaneously across buy-side and sell-side mandates.

 - Strong analytical and financial skills, with a deep understanding of accounting principles and commercial drivers.

 - Track record in delivering actionable insights and value creation strategies post-deal.

 - Exceptional written and verbal communication, including senior stakeholder engagement and reporting.

 - Entrepreneurial mindset with the agility to thrive in a non-hierarchical, fast-growth environment.

 - Qualified accountant (ACA, ACCA, CIMA or equivalent) highly preferred.




NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience in Private Equity environments or PE-backed portfolio companies.

 - Exposure to international transactions and cross-border coordination.

 - Familiarity with AI, data-driven diligence tools, or operational analytics platforms.

 - Sector experience in B2B services, technology, or consumer markets.




WORKING AT UNITY ADVISORY

At Unity Advisory, you’ll be at the fore

source_jobs.title: Senior Manager - Deals Analytics
source_jobs.description:
Unity Advisory is a pure-play, experience-led CFO advisory firm providing integrated finance, commercial, tax and deals support. We are free from audit conflicts and AI-enabled by design, combining deep technical expertise with automation, analytics and AI built into the delivery of every engagement. We are building an alternative to traditional advisory firms, focused on clarity over complexity, outcomes over hours, and practical advice delivered at pace for CFOs making high-consequence decisions.



We are now looking for a Senior Manager – Deals Analytics to join our growing team.



The Role

As a Deals and Value Creation Senior Manager in the Deals team, you will play a pivotal role in delivering advanced analytics and strategic insights to help our clients make better financial and commercial decisions throughout the deal lifecycle. You will be supporting both buy-side and sell-side transactions and delivering post deal value creation style projects, through the deployment of advanced analytics and AI, leveraging your commercial expertise and deals know-how.

The Deals unit specialises in Deal Readiness, Value Creation, Decision Support, and Reporting Insights. In this role, you will lead projects across these areas challenging the status quo, harnessing cutting-edge data analysis (including AI/ LLM tools), and collaborating closely with clients, other advisors and colleagues to drive tangible results.

This role will also be pivotal in supporting our FDD team to reimagine the world of diligence, reducing reliance on low-value reporting and increasing client commercial value delivered through insightful analytics that evidence what matters most.

This is a client-facing, hands-on position in a fast-growing start-up/ scale-up environment, offering the chance to work on high-impact engagements and help build a new kind of consultancy from the ground up.



What You’ll Do

 - Critically assess and enhance clients’ exit readiness by challenging the equity story, data landscape, KPIs, and business plan reporting to maximise exit value for clients preparing for a sale or IPO.

 - Bring a critical eye and a commercial mind to both buy-side and sell-side diligence projects. Rapidly assess and present drivers of value to support or critically test the deal hypothesis using leading analytics. Enhancing the impact of diligence processes.

 - Use commercial analytics to identify and unlock profitability improvements (e.g., customer/ product profitability, SKU rationalisation, pricing strategy, site/ network optimisation).

 - Apply advanced analytics, including forecasting, scenario modelling, and predictive modelling to support better financial and commercial decision-making.

 - Develop and automate clear, impactful board and management reporting solutions, including dashboards and reports that provide business unit-level insights with market context.

 - Champion the use of AI and data-driven techniques in all projects, leveraging tools such as large language models (LLMs) and other analytics platforms.

 - Work closely within cross-functional teams and engage directly with Unity Advisory Partners, coordinating project workstreams in a highly collaborative environment.

 - Translate complex data findings into clear, compelling recommendations for senior stakeholders, frequently presenting insights and reports to C-level clients.



What You Bring

 - ACA, CIMA, or equivalent accounting/ finance certification.

 - Proven experience in a consulting role (ideally Big Four or boutique), with a track record of delivering measurable client impact.

 - Hands-on experience in deals, transactions, or M&A (e.g., transaction services, due diligence, private equity portfolio support).

 - Exceptional data management and analytical capability, including working with large datasets, financial models, and BI/ analytics tooling.

 - Keen interest or exposure to artificial intelligence and large language models, with the willingness to learn and

source_jobs.title: Senior Manager - R&D Tax (Tax & Costing)
source_jobs.description:
Unity Advisory is a pure-play, experience-led CFO advisory firm providing integrated finance, commercial, tax and deals support. We are free from audit conflicts and AI-enabled by design, combining deep technical expertise with automation, analytics and AI built into the delivery of every engagement. We are building an alternative to traditional advisory firms, focused on clarity over complexity, outcomes over hours, and practical advice delivered at pace for CFOs making high-consequence decisions.



The Role

We’re looking for a commercially sharp, technically rigorous Senior Manager to join our fast-growing R&D tax practice. This role sits at the heart of claim delivery, owning the financial and tax-technical dimensions of every engagement, from cost identification and apportionment through to HMRC-ready computations and submissions.

You’ll lead the quantification of R&D expenditure across a varied portfolio of clients, from scaling tech companies to large enterprise groups, ensuring claims are robust, defensible, and fully optimised within the boundaries of HMRC guidance. Working closely with Partners and the Technology & Science team, you’ll bring analytical precision to complex cost structures and play a central role in shaping how we embed AI and automation into our costing and compliance workflows.

This is not a pure compliance role. You will be expected to think critically about how R&D tax costing is done today, challenge legacy methodologies, and actively build or improve AI-driven workflows that raise the bar on efficiency, accuracy, and client experience.



What You’ll Do

 - Own the end-to-end financial and tax-technical delivery of R&D tax claims, including cost identification, apportionment, computation, CT600 adjustments, and HMRC submission.

 - Analyse client cost bases to identify and quantify qualifying direct and indirect R&D expenditure under the merged RDEC scheme and the Enhanced R&D Intensive Support (ERIS) regime.

 - Lead detailed cost reviews with finance teams, interrogating trial balances, payroll data, subcontractor arrangements, cloud computing and data licence costs, and overhead allocation methodologies.

 - Navigate the complexities of the merged scheme, including the overseas expenditure restrictions, PAYE/NIC cap mechanics, subsidised expenditure rules, and claim notification requirements.

 - Prepare and review robust claim reports and supporting schedules that withstand HMRC enquiry, including the Additional Information Form (AIF).

 - Stay ahead of legislative and case-law developments, advising clients on their practical impact and adjusting claim strategies accordingly.

 - Work alongside the Technology & Science team to ensure qualifying activity narratives and cost schedules are fully aligned.

 - Manage HMRC enquiries and voluntary disclosures, acting as the primary tax-technical point of contact.

 - Develop and maintain trust-based senior client relationships, advising CFOs, Group Tax Directors, and Financial Controllers.

 - Provide leadership, coaching, and technical oversight to junior team members on costing methodology and tax compliance.

 - Actively adopt, build, and improve AI-driven tools and workflows for cost analysis, claim computation, and compliance, in line with Unity’s culture of embedding AI across all workstreams.

 - • Contribute to business development, pricing strategy, thought leadership, and go-to-market initiatives.



What You Bring

 - Deep expertise in the tax and financial mechanics of UK R&D tax relief, including RDEC, ERIS, cost categorisation, and CT600 treatment, ideally gained in a Big 4, top-tier, or specialist R&D advisory firm.

 - Proven ability to navigate complex corporate cost structures, group recharges, subcontractor chains, and multi-entity claim scenarios.

 - Strong working knowledge of HMRC’s R&D compliance framework, enquiry processes, and the Additional Information Form.

 - Excellent project management skills with the ability to

source_jobs.title: Senior Manager - Capital Markets & Accounting Advisory
source_jobs.description:
About Unity Advisory

Unity Advisory is a pure-play, experience-led CFO advisory firm providing integrated finance, commercial, tax and deals support. We are free from audit conflicts and AI-enabled by design, combining deep technical expertise with automation, analytics and AI built into the delivery of every engagement. We are building an alternative to traditional advisory firms, focused on clarity over complexity, outcomes over hours, and practical advice delivered at pace for CFOs making high-consequence decisions.



The Role

The Capital Markets & Accounting Advisory team at Unity provides ‘end-to-end’ support for companies on their transaction and IPO journeys. This includes IFRS & US GAAP, Regulatory and exchange rules and regulations, carve-out and pro forma reporting, deal structuring and PMO, internal controls (including SOX), and post-IPO public company advice and support.

This is a hands-on role. You will own key workstreams across readiness, execution, and post-transaction support. You will lead delivery on capital markets transactions and accounting advisory engagements.You will work across the exchange and regulatory requirements, IFRS & US GAAP and the myriad of other stakeholder reporting requirements.

We take a tech-first approach to delivery. You will use data, automation, and standardised tools to drive efficiency and reduce manual work. You will be expected to challenge traditional ways of working and improve how engagements are delivered. If you are ready to take on some of our clients’ most complex challenges, this is the role for you.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Build strong working relationships with clients

 - Lead accounting workstreams

 - Act as the main point of contact between clients, auditors, and other advisors

 - Prepare and review financial information

 - Take ownership of technical accounting positions across complex areas

 - Support GAAP conversions

 - Design and implement control frameworks, including SOX

 - Use technology and automation to streamline reporting and delivery

 - Improve processes and reduce manual effort

 - Manage timelines and deliverables

 - Review outputs and ensure quality

 - Support and develop junior team members


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - Qualified accountant, ACA, ACCA, CPA

 - Strong accounting, auditing and reporting experience

 - Excellent knowledge of US GAAP and IFRS

 - Deep experience working on transactions and accounting advisory

 - Comfortable leading delivery and managing teams

 - Interest in improving processes and using technology

 - Able to work directly with senior client stakeholders

 - Clear and practical communicator

Additional Information
At Unity Advisory, we are committed to providing an inclusive and accessible recruitment process. In line with the Equality Act 2010, we will accommodate any suitable candidate requiring assistance to attend or conduct an interview. If you need any adjustments or support, please let us know when either scheduling your interview or in your application cover letter. We are dedicated to ensuring that everyone has an equal opportunity to succeed and are here to support you throughout the process.



PLEASE BE ADVISED THAT WE DO NOT ACCEPT ANY UNSOLICITED CVS FROM THIRD PARTIES.

source_jobs.title: Manager- FDD & Deals Analytics
source_jobs.description:
About Unity Advisory

Unity Advisory is a pure-play, experience-led CFO advisory firm providing integrated finance, commercial, tax and deals support. We are free from audit conflicts and AI-enabled by design, combining deep technical expertise with automation, analytics and AI built into the delivery of every engagement. We are building an alternative to traditional advisory firms, focused on clarity over complexity, outcomes over hours, and practical advice delivered at pace for CFOs making high-consequence decisions.

The role

This is a dual-capability role sitting at the intersection of Financial Due Diligence and Deals Analytics. You’ll contribute to FDD engagements — assessing quality of earnings, working capital, and transaction risks — and you’ll bring the analytical firepower to go beyond the standard diligence output.

Day to day, that means interrogating client data, building models and dashboards that tell a clearer commercial story, and applying AI and advanced analytics to problems that traditional diligence hasn’t solved well. You’ll work closely with Unity’s Deals Partners and Senior Managers, and directly with clients from day one.



What you’ll do

 - Support buy-side and sell-side Financial Due Diligence, assessing quality of earnings, cash flows, working capital, and transaction risks — and helping to shape the commercial story behind the numbers.

 - Use data-driven techniques to rapidly identify and present drivers of value, test the deal hypothesis, and elevate the impact of diligence work.

 - Help clients prepare for sale or IPO by interrogating their data landscape, KPIs, and business plan reporting — and identifying where the numbers need to work harder.

 - Build and automate clear, impactful management reporting tools — dashboards and board-level outputs that give clients real commercial visibility.

 - Use LLMs, forecasting, scenario modelling, and predictive analytics to solve commercial problems — not as an experiment, but as standard practice.

 - Take large, messy datasets and translate them into clear, compelling recommendations for senior stakeholders, including C-suite clients.

 - Contribute to value creation projects post-deal — including profitability analysis, KPI redesign, and operating model analytics for PE-backed portfolio companies.



What you’ll bring

 - ACA, CIMA, or equivalent finance/accounting qualification.

 - Experience in a consulting or advisory role — Big Four or boutique — with a track record of delivering in deals or transaction environments.

 - Hands-on exposure to FDD, transaction services, or M&A — you understand the deal process and what clients need from it.

 - Strong analytical capability — comfortable working with large, complex datasets, financial models, and BI/analytics tools.

 - Genuine curiosity about AI and how it applies in deals contexts. You don’t need to be a developer, but you’re interested, you’ve experimented, and you want to build that muscle.

 - Clear, confident communication — able to present data-driven findings to senior stakeholders in a way that lands.



Nice to have

 - Proficiency in SQL and/or Python.

 - Experience with Power BI, Databricks, Alteryx, or similar platforms.

 - Excel financial modelling experience, including three-statement forecasts.

 - Exposure to PE value creation work — performance improvement, KPI redesign, or operating model analytics.
   
   

Additional Information
At Unity Advisory, we are committed to providing an inclusive and accessible recruitment process. In line with the Equality Act 2010, we will accommodate any suitable candidate requiring assistance to attend or conduct an interview. If you need any adjustments or support, please let us know when either scheduling your interview or in your application cover letter. We are dedicated to ensuring that everyone has an equal opportunity to succeed and are here to support you throughout the process.

 

PLEASE BE ADVISED THAT WE DO NOT ACCEPT ANY UNSOL

source_jobs.title: Private Client Manager
source_jobs.description:
About Unity Advisory

Unity Advisory is a pure-play, experience-led CFO advisory platform. Built for the AI-enabled world, we operate a lean, conflict-free, and client-centric model that embeds AI across all workstreams. With no audit practice, we are free from the conflicts that constrain the traditional Big Four — which means we focus entirely on delivering value to clients with agility and outcome-based commercial models. Our culture is highly collaborative, flat-structured and free of traditional partner P&L silos.

Shape [data:image/png;base64,iVBORw0KGgoAAAANSUhEUgAAAAEAAAABCAYAAAAfFcSJAAAAAXNSR0IArs4c6QAAAARnQU1BAACxjwv8YQUAAAAJcEhZcwAADsMAAA7DAcdvqGQAAAANSURBVBhXY2BgYGAAAAAFAAGKM+MAAAAAAElFTkSuQmCC]

The Role

We are growing our tax capability and looking for a Private Client Tax Manager to join the team.

Our clients are entrepreneurs, business owners, high-net-worth individuals and their families. We want someone who thinks about how technologycan help them help their clients — someone curious about how AI, automation and modern tax tools can make delivery of advice sharper and give advisors more time to spend with clients to gain the greatest understanding of their affairs.

If you are the kind of person who has looked at a manual process and thought “there has to be a better way,” you will fit in here!

Shape [data:image/png;base64,iVBORw0KGgoAAAANSUhEUgAAAAEAAAABCAYAAAAfFcSJAAAAAXNSR0IArs4c6QAAAARnQU1BAACxjwv8YQUAAAAJcEhZcwAADsMAAA7DAcdvqGQAAAANSURBVBhXY2BgYGAAAAAFAAGKM+MAAAAAAElFTkSuQmCC]

Responsibilities

 - Support the team in managing a portfolio of private clients including HNWIs, entrepreneurs, trusts and estates across income tax, CGT and IHT advisory work and associated compliance.

 - Support partners and senior colleagues on advisory matters including shareholder tax planning, tax residency , succession planning, and transactions.

 - Manage self-assessment tax return preparation and delivery alongside related compliance obligations.

 - Liaise directly with clients and HMRC, handling correspondence and enquiries with confidence

 - Identify opportunities to use technology and automation to improve how work is delivered — and actively push for those improvements

 - Contribute to business development activity including proposals and client meetings

Shape [data:image/png;base64,iVBORw0KGgoAAAANSUhEUgAAAAEAAAABCAYAAAAfFcSJAAAAAXNSR0IArs4c6QAAAARnQU1BAACxjwv8YQUAAAAJcEhZcwAADsMAAA7DAcdvqGQAAAANSURBVBhXY2BgYGAAAAAFAAGKM+MAAAAAAElFTkSuQmCC]

What You’ll Bring

 - ACA, ATT or CTA qualified

 - 3+ years of private client tax experience, ideally in a professional services or advisory environment

 - Real technical knowledge across income tax, CGT and IHT; trust experience is a plus

 - Genuine interest in how technology can improve tax delivery — familiarity with existing tools is useful; intellectual curiosity about AI and automation matters more

 - Strong communicator — comfortable dealing directly with clients and explaining complex positions clearly

 - Organised and dependable, able to manage your own workload and client deadlines

 - Commercial awareness and an appetite to grow with the firm, learning from the expert team around you.

Shape [data:image/png;base64,iVBORw0KGgoAAAANSUhEUgAAAAEAAAABCAYAAAAfFcSJAAAAAXNSR0IArs4c6QAAAARnQU1BAACxjwv8YQUAAAAJcEhZcwAADsMAAA7DAcdvqGQAAAANSURBVBhXY2BgYGAAAAAFAAGKM+MAAAAAAElFTkSuQmCC]

Work Environment

A truly hybrid and flexible working environment. We offer the opportunity to be at the forefront of AI-driven advisory services. You’ll be working on cutting-edge projects and encouraged to deploy the latest analytical tools and approaches. This is a fantastic opportunity to join a fast-growing business and shape your career.

Shape [data:image/png;base64,iVBORw0KGgoAAAANSUhEUgAAAAEAAAABCAYAAAAfFcSJAAAAAXNSR0IArs4c6QAAAARnQU1BAACxjwv8YQUAAAAJcEhZcwAADsMAAA7DAcdvqGQAAAANSURBVBhXY2BgYGAAAAAFAAGKM+MAAAAAAElFTkSuQmCC]

At Unity Advisory, we are co

source_jobs.title: Finance Transformation Senior Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT UNITY ADVISORY

Unity Advisory is a pure-play, experience-led CFO advisory firm providing integrated finance, commercial, tax and deals support. We are free from audit conflicts and AI-enabled by design, combining deep technical expertise with automation, analytics and AI built into the delivery of every engagement. We are building an alternative to traditional advisory firms, focused on clarity over complexity, outcomes over hours, and practical advice delivered at pace for CFOs making high-consequence decisions.




THE ROLE

As a Finance Transformation Senior Manager, you will own the end-to-end delivery of complex transformation programmes — leading cross-functional teams, holding the client relationship at a senior level, and taking accountability for outcomes, not just outputs.

This is a role for someone who is ready to step beyond workstream management and operate as the day-to-day face of Unity Advisory on the engagements they lead. You will shape how we solve client problems, contribute directly to growing our CFO advisory practice, and play a real part in building the firm — including developing the people around you. At a firm without the structural constraints of a Big Four, that means your influence and impact will be felt faster.




RESPONSIBILITIES

• Lead end-to-end delivery of large-scale finance transformation programmes across process, data and technology.

• Act as the senior day-to-day client relationship holder, managing CFO-level stakeholders with confidence and building long-term trusted advisor relationships.

• Shape transformation roadmaps and future-state finance operating models from initial diagnostic through to implementation.

• Oversee redesign of core finance processes including R2R, P2P, O2C and FP&A, ensuring solutions are practical and built to last.

• Lead delivery of cloud ERP and EPM implementations, automation programmes, data platforms and AI-enabled analytics solutions.

• Take accountability for project commercial management — scope, budget, resourcing and risk — across engagements.

• Lead executive workshops, steer steering committee updates, and present findings and recommendations at board level.

• Drive business development activity including client targeting, proposal leadership and pitches — bringing in new work and expanding existing relationships.

• Contribute to building Unity Advisory’s CFO Office proposition, thought leadership and go-to-market approach.

• Manage, mentor and develop Managers and more junior team members, taking an active role in performance and career development.

• Support the firm’s recruitment and capability-building as we grow.






WHAT YOU’LL BRING

• 8+ years of experience in finance transformation, including significant time in a consulting or advisory environment.

• A strong track record of owning and delivering complex, large-scale transformation programmes — from diagnostic through to sustained adoption.

• Senior client relationship experience, with the confidence and credibility to advise at CFO and C-suite level.

• Deep expertise in ERP and EPM platforms, process re-engineering, operating model design, and data and analytics.

• A proven ability to lead, develop and inspire teams — including managing performance and supporting career progression.

• Commercial acumen and prior experience contributing to business development, proposals and client growth.

• Finance or accounting qualification: ACA, ACCA, CIMA or CPA.






WORK ENVIRONMENT

A truly hybrid and flexible working environment. We offer the opportunity to be at the forefront of AI-driven advisory services. You’ll be working on cutting-edge projects and encouraged to deploy the latest analytical tools and approaches.




ADDITIONAL INFORMATION

At Unity Advisory, we are committed to providing an inclusive and accessible recruitment process. In line with the Equality Act 2010, we will accommodate any suitable candidate requiring assistance to attend or conduct an inte

source_jobs.title: Technical Recruiter
source_jobs.description:
ROLE OVERVIEW

We are seeking a Technical Recruiter on a 6 month fixed term contract to accelerate hiring across AI/ML, engineering, data and cloud/DevOps. This is a delivery-led position with strong stakeholder exposure, requiring advanced sourcing, technical fluency and pace.


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Deliver full-cycle hiring across:
   
   - AI/ML Engineering & Applied Science
   
   - Full Stack & Backend
   
   - Data Engineering, Analytics & Platform
   
   - DevOps, SRE, Cloud Infrastructure
   
   - Product, Architecture & Security

 - Build targeted technical talent pipelines using:
   
   - Ashby ATS & LinkedIn Recruiter
   
   - GitHub, HuggingFace, Stack Overflow, Kaggle, Discord, ArXiv
   
   - Modern sourcing automations (e.g., PhantomBuster)

 - Execute structured screening aligned to technical competency scorecards.

 - Maintain recruitment funnel hygiene and reporting (conversion, time-to-fill).

 - Develop outbound campaigns tailored to engineering and AI communities.

 - Partner with hiring stakeholders on prioritisation and forecasting.

 - Ensure a consistently high-touch candidate experience.


EXPERIENCE & SKILLS

Must-have

 - 4–8+ years technical recruiting with specialism in:
   
   - AI/ML
   
   - Full stack / backend engineering
   
   - Cloud / DevOps / platform
   
   - Data engineering / data science

 - Strong ability to speak credibly on:
   
   - Model lifecycle, model ops, inference & deployment
   
   - Data pipelines, ETL, warehousing & lakehouse patterns
   
   - CI/CD, IaC and cloud architecture

 - Evidence of delivering high-complexity tech hiring within short timeframes.

 - Sourcing capability beyond mainstream channels.

Nice-to-have

 - Experience in scaling advisory, consultancy or PE-backed environments.

 - Familiarity with Ashby ATS and Microsoft Teams workflows.

 - Exposure to building technical scorecards and interview loops.


CONTRACT OUTCOMES (FIRST 6 MONTHS)

 - Priority engineering, AI and data roles successfully filled against agreed SLAs.

 - Predictive pipeline built for recurring strategic tech profiles.

 - Standardised competency-based interview process embedded with hiring teams.

 - Market mapping completed for AI/ML and cloud talent communities.


CONTRACT STRUCTURE

 - Type: Fixed Term Contract (FTC)

 - Length: 6 months with potential extension based on business needs

 - Hours: Full-time

 - Start: ASAP

 - Compensation: Competitive FTC package


WHY JOIN US

This is a pivotal period of growth for Unity, and technical hiring sits at the core of our client delivery and market differentiation. You’ll own a mission-critical hiring portfolio, partner directly with senior leadership, and shape how Unity engages technical talent.

source_jobs.title: People Coordinator
source_jobs.description:
Are you an ambitious HR professional looking for a role where you can genuinely make your mark? This could be exactly the opportunity you've been waiting for.

Unity Advisory is a challenger advisory firm built for the AI-enabled world: lean, conflict-free, and client-centric. We embed AI across everything we do, and our culture is collaborative, flat-structured, and built on shared success. We're growing fast, and we need great people to help us do it well.

The Role

We're looking for a People Coordinator to join our People Team on a permanent basis. This is a newly created role, which means you'll have real influence over how it takes shape. We're looking to move quickly, so if you're available to start soon, even better.

Day to day, you'll be the operational heartbeat of the People function, supporting the full employee lifecycle from contracts and onboarding through to payroll, benefits, absence, and everything in between. You'll work in our HRIS, produce people data and insights, and help us keep our processes sharp, documented, and ready to scale.

This isn't a role where you'll sit in a corner processing forms. You'll be front and centre: the first point of contact for employees and managers, a trusted member of a lean team, and someone with a direct line of sight to the impact your work has on the business.

What We're Looking For

You'll be organised, accurate, and genuinely passionate about people and HR. You'll bring some prior experience in an HR, a solid grasp of UK employment fundamentals, and the kind of proactive, can-do energy that means you spot what needs doing and get on with it.

A CIPD qualification (or studying towards one) would be a bonus, as would experience with HRIS platforms or a background in a startup or scale-up environment, but we care more about mindset than CV boxes. If you're curious, collaborative, and ready to grow, we want to hear from you.

This role is based in our London office a minimum of four days a week. There may be scope for one day of flexibility depending on the needs of the business at the time.

If you're motivated by impact, variety, and being part of something that's building with purpose, and you can hit the ground running, we'd love to hear from you.

source_jobs.title: GTM Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Unity Advisory

Unity Advisory is a challenger advisory firm. Built for the AI-enabled world, we operate a lean, conflict-free model that embeds AI across all workstreams. Free from audit conflicts, focused entirely on high-impact outcomes for CFOs making high-consequence decisions. We're scaling fast and building the commercial infrastructure to match.

The Role

This is a builder role. You'll help design, build and maintain the systems that power Unity Advisory's go-to-market engine — the data pipelines, outreach infrastructure, enrichment workflows and automation that gets us in front of the right CFOs and PE deal teams at the right moment.

You think in code and workflows, not spreadsheets. You have hands-on experience with GTM tooling, know how to work with APIs, and are comfortable in a terminal as well as a slide deck. You'll work closely with senior stakeholders to keep the commercial machine fast, accurate and converting.

Responsibilities

 - Support the build and maintenance of automated pipelines that enrich, segment and score target lists — CFOs at PE-backed businesses, finance leaders across our core sectors.

 - Integrate data sources (Companies House, Dealroom, LinkedIn, news feeds) to surface trigger events: funding rounds, leadership changes, new deals.

 - Build and manage multi-channel outreach sequences across email and LinkedIn using tools like Clay, Apollo or Instantly.

 - Write and iterate on outreach copy — specific, well-timed, and nothing like a template.

 - Automate follow-up cadences and meeting scheduling so senior team members stay focused on conversations, not admin.

 - Contribute to the GTM tech stack — CRM, enrichment tools, sequencing platforms, AI tooling.

 - Build and maintain reporting dashboards that show what's been sent, what's landed and what's converted.

 - Identify and flag pipeline bottlenecks and implement fixes programmatically.

What You'll Bring

 - 3–5 years of experience working with GTM systems in a B2B or SaaS environment.

 - Solid coding ability — Python, JavaScript or similar. Comfortable building lightweight tools when off-the-shelf doesn't cut it.

 - Hands-on experience with the modern GTM stack: Clay, Apollo, HubSpot, Instantly, LinkedIn Sales Nav, and AI tooling for enrichment and content generation.

 - Comfortable with APIs, webhooks and no-code/low-code tools (Zapier, Make, n8n).

 - Good written communication — you can write outreach copy as well as you can write code.

 - Organised, structured and able to manage your own workload with minimal supervision.
   
   

Nice to Have

 - Familiarity with the CFO, finance buyer or PE landscape.

 - Exposure to ABM, pipeline analytics or event-based campaign execution.

 - Experience contributing to a GTM function build-out.
   
   

Why This Role?

Real ownership, high visibility and the chance to see the direct impact of what you build. You'll work closely with a fast-moving team and play a meaningful role in shaping how a growing advisory firm goes to market. We're building something different — and we want people who want to build it with us.





We are committed to an inclusive recruitment process. If you need any adjustments at any stage, please let us know.

We do not accept unsolicited CVs from third party agencies.

notes: Unity Advisory is a pure-play, experience-led CFO advisory firm with a strong focus on AI and automation. The company is growing rapidly and hiring across various roles, including technical positions.
